name: Dismissal of Montazeri as Supreme Leader Successor
description: On 28 March 1989, [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) formally stripped [Hossein-Ali Montazeri](/people/hossein-ali-montazeri) of his position as designated successor to the Supreme Leadership. The dismissal followed Montazeri's sustained opposition to the [1988 mass executions](/events/mass-executions-1988) of political prisoners and his public criticisms of the revolutionary establishment. [Ahmad Khomeini](/people/ahmad-khomeini), who controlled access to his father, and [Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani](/people/akbar-hashemi-rafsanjani), then Speaker of Parliament, played key roles in Montazeri's isolation. In his letter of dismissal, Khomeini accused Montazeri of being manipulated by 'hypocrites' — the regime's term for the Mojahedin-e Khalq. With Montazeri removed, the [1989 constitutional revision](/events/constitutional-revision-1989) dropped the requirement that the Supreme Leader be a marja-e taqlid, enabling [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) — then only a Hojatoleslam — to assume the position after Khomeini's death in June 1989.
editorial_notes: Khomeini's letter dated 28 March 1989. Constitutional revision followed in April-July 1989. Montazeri had been designated successor by Assembly of Experts in November 1985.
